input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan for .NET MAUI on Windows 11 (Android + Windows targets):

---

## 1. Install .NET SDK

```powershell
# Check if already installed
dotnet --list-sdks

# Install via winget (discovers latest stable .NET 8/9 SDK)
winget search Microsoft.DotNet.SDK
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9
```

## 2. Install MAUI Workload

```powershell
# List available workloads to confirm name
dotnet workload search maui

# Install MAUI (includes Android, Windows sub-workloads)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify
dotnet workload list
```

## 3. Install Visual Studio Build Tools (Windows target needs WinUI/UWP SDK)

```powershell
# Option A: Full VS (recommended — includes designer, hot reload, emulator mgmt)
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.Community.2022

# Then modify to add these workloads via CLI:
& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installershell.exe" modify `
  --installPath "C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\Community" `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.Maui.All `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.ManagedDesktop `
  --quiet --wait

# Option B: Minimal — just Build Tools (CI/headless scenarios)
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.BuildTools.2022
```

## 4. Android SDK Setup

The MAUI workload bundles Android SDK pieces, but you may need to accept licenses and ensure the right API levels:

```powershell
# Find where the Android SDK was placed by the workload
$androidSdk = Join-Path $env:LOCALAPPDATA "Microsoft\Android\sdk"
if (!(Test-Path $androidSdk)) {
    # Fallback: check dotnet's android workload manifest
    $androidSdk = Join-Path $env:USERPROFILE ".android\sdk"
}
Write-Host "Android SDK at: $androidSdk"

# Discover installed platforms & tools
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list

# Accept all licenses (required before first build)
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --licenses

# Install a specific API level if needed (discover available ones first)
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list | Select-String "platforms;android-"
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" "platforms;android-35" "build-tools;35.0.0"
```

## 5. Android Emulator (optional but useful)

```powershell
# Install emulator + system image
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" "emulator" "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64"

# Create an AVD
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat" create avd `
  -n "Pixel_MAUI" -k "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" -d "pixel_7"

# Enable Windows Hypervisor Platform (run as Admin) for fast emulation
Enable-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName "HypervisorPlatform" -All -NoRestart
```

## 6. Windows App SDK / WinUI3 (Windows target)

```powershell
# Usually bundled with the VS MAUI workload. Verify:
winget list Microsoft.WindowsAppSDK
# If missing:
winget search Microsoft.WindowsAppSDK
winget install Microsoft.WindowsAppSDK

# Enable Developer Mode (required for sideloading, run as Admin)
reg add "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
  /t REG_DWORD /f /v "AllowDevelopment‌Sideloading" /d 1
reg add "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
  /t REG_DWORD /f /v "AllowAllTrustedApps" /d 1
# Or: Settings → System → For developers → Developer Mode = On
```

## 7. Create & Build a Test Project

```powershell
# Scaffold
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Build for Windows (WinUI3)
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Run on Windows
dotnet run -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Run on Android emulator (start emulator first)
dotnet build -f net9.0-android -t:Install -t:Run -p:AndroidAttachDebugger=false
```

## 8. Troubleshooting / Verification

```powershell
# Full diagnostic check
dotnet workload list
dotnet --info

# Check if JDK is found (MAUI bundles one, but verify)
dotnet build -f net9.0-android -v:diag 2>&1 | Select-String "JavaSdkDirectory"

# Repair workloads if something is off
dotnet workload repair
```

---

**Key things to note:**
- Replace `net9.0` with `net8.0` if you prefer LTS
- API level `android-35` — discover the right one with `sdkmanager --list`
- The Windows TFM (`net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0`) must match an installed Windows SDK version; `19041` is the common minimum
data.reasoningText: The user wants a complete step-by-step plan for setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 targeting Android and Windows. They can't run commands now, so they want all the PowerShell commands laid out. Let me provide a thorough, practical guide.
